title: T-060 — UI: Pipeline Table + Split views (TableView + SplitView prototypes) (NPD-f)
prompt:
# T-060 — Pipeline Table + Split views UI

PRD: 01-NPD-PRD.md §17.8, §17.12 + UX 01-NPD-UX.md pipeline_table + pipeline_split
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira
Prototype: design/Monopilot Design System/npd/pipeline.jsx:54-132 (TableView + SplitView)

## Goal
Two additional pipeline view modes: TableView (sortable cols Code/Name/Type/CurrentGate/Priority/Owner/TargetLaunch/Progress with URL persistence ?sort&dir) and SplitView (left compact table + right ProjectDetail panel + URL ?selected=) — switched via ?view=table|split.

## Implementation contract
1. apps/web/app/(npd)/pipeline/_components/table-view.tsx ('use client') uses shadcn Table + Badge + sortable headers; URL query persistence via Next.js router.
2. apps/web/app/(npd)/pipeline/_components/split-view.tsx ('use client') two-pane layout (left list, right ProjectDetail panel sticky); selection via ?selected=projectId; min-width 1280 px breakpoint falls back to table.
3. apps/web/app/(npd)/pipeline/page.tsx switches between Kanban/Table/Split based on ?view= search param (default kanban).
4. RTL tests for both views: column rendering + sort + URL persistence + selection.

## Files
- apps/web/app/(npd)/pipeline/_components/table-view.tsx [create]
- apps/web/app/(npd)/pipeline/_components/split-view.tsx [create]
- apps/web/app/(npd)/pipeline/_components/project-detail-panel.tsx [create]
- apps/web/app/(npd)/pipeline/_components/__tests__/table-view.test.tsx [create]
- apps/web/app/(npd)/pipeline/_components/__tests__/split-view.test.tsx [create]
- apps/web/app/(npd)/pipeline/page.tsx [modify]
- design/Monopilot Design System/npd/pipeline.jsx [ref]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given the production TableView renders, when compared to design/Monopilot Design System/npd/pipeline.jsx:54-88 (TableView), then it has the same 8 columns (Code, Name, Type, Current Gate, Priority, Owner, Target Launch, Progress) using shadcn Table + Badge primitives — verified by RTL snapshot + parity checklist
2. Given the production SplitView renders, when compared to design/Monopilot Design System/npd/pipeline.jsx:90-132 (SplitView), then it shows a left compact list + right ProjectDetail sticky panel with selection persisted via URL ?selected= per §17.12 split mode contract
3. Given URL ?view=split&selected=PRJ-123, when the page loads, then SplitView mounts with PRJ-123 pre-selected and the right panel shows its detail per §17.12 URL persistence

## Test strategy
- RED: RTL tests for both views asserting parity + sort + selection persistence before components exist
- Run 'pnpm --filter web test table-view split-view'

## Risk red lines
- Do not use HTML <table> sort attributes — use shadcn Table primitives
- Do not paste prototype JSX verbatim
- Do not break the responsive 1280 px breakpoint contract
